corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: طَبَعَ is a finite perfect verb; the row needs exact form, voice, subject/context, and learner-visible finite wording before an authored hover can be certified
drill_prompt: Given طَبَعَ, identify why the entry infinitive is not a token hover and name the finite-form fields required before review.
learner_explanation: طَبَعَ contributes a completed action in its āyah, not the dictionary label “to seal one’s heart”.
nahw_lesson: Clause context can choose the English wording, but it cannot turn a finite token into an infinitive hover.
sarf_lesson: For finite verbs, record aspect, voice, person, number, gender, and form before accepting entry prose.
what_failed: A populated hover reused entry infinitive prose instead of the exact finite verb contribution.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ٱلْمُسْتَعَانُ is a nominal/passive-participle-like surface; do not certify from a bare assist infinitive without POS, derivative type, and referent review
drill_prompt: Classify ٱلْمُسْتَعَانُ as host surface, derivative/POS, and explain why “to assist” fails as a hover.
learner_explanation: ٱلْمُسْتَعَانُ is not a finite “to assist” token. The form points to the one sought for help / help-sought role by context.
nahw_lesson: Referent and clause role decide whether the public wording is an attribute, title, or noun phrase.
sarf_lesson: Definite مُـ nominal or participial rows need derivative/POS classification before any verb-family reuse.
what_failed: A verb entry family supplied a token hover for a nominal surface.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: لِيَغِيظَ has lām/governor review plus a finite host; component evidence from غيظ cannot certify the whole token
drill_prompt: For لِيَغِيظَ, list the component candidate and explain why it remains a blocker row.
learner_explanation: The token begins with لِـ and contains a finite verb, so the learner needs the lām function and host form, not only the root-family meaning.
nahw_lesson: A lām attached to a verb can govern function/mood and must be reviewed before hover trust.
sarf_lesson: Component candidates must stay separate from whole-token candidates and cannot weaken the gate.
what_failed: Component candidate evidence was useful for routing but not whole-token proof.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: بِغَيْظِكُمْ requires bāʾ + host noun + كُمْ suffix and PP/context review; a verb-family anger gloss cannot certify it
drill_prompt: Segment بِغَيْظِكُمْ and name the blocker that prevents whole-token propagation.
learner_explanation: بِغَيْظِكُمْ includes بِـ and كُمْ. A learner should not see only an anger/enrage family gloss.
nahw_lesson: The prepositional relation and attachment determine the English contribution and require context review.
sarf_lesson: A bāʾ-prefixed nominal host with suffix must preserve preposition, host, and suffix as separate visible pieces.
what_failed: A component/root-family candidate hid the written preposition and suffix-bearing host.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: وَارِدَهُمْ requires exact POS plus the هُمْ suffix before a learner-safe token hover can be certified
drill_prompt: Segment وَارِدَهُمْ and explain why the suffix blocks a bare entry gloss.
learner_explanation: The final هُمْ contributes “their/them” by context. It cannot disappear behind the host family meaning.
nahw_lesson: The suffix relation and referent determine whether the English is possessive, object, or contextual phrase wording.
sarf_lesson: Suffix-bearing verb or nominal hosts need the suffix in parse, display segments, and learner explanation.
visible_bad_hover: to arrive at flowing water or the Fire
what_failed: Entry-family prose was shown without accounting for the attached plural suffix.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: فَدَمَّرْنَٰهُمْ contains فَ, finite Form II/perfect-looking host, and هُمْ object; component destroy evidence is not whole-token proof
drill_prompt: Explain why فَدَمَّرْنَٰهُمْ remains component-only until the whole token is certified.
learner_explanation: فَدَمَّرْنَٰهُمْ is not just “to destroy”; it contains “so/then”, a finite “We destroyed” host, and “them”.
nahw_lesson: The fāʾ relation and object referent may affect the learner wording and require compatible reasoning.
sarf_lesson: Finite destruction-family rows need form, subject, and suffix/object analysis before hover trust.
what_failed: The row found a useful host-family candidate but lacked whole-token function, form, and object proof.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: حَثِيثًۭا may need only rich segment metadata if its wording survives review; do not treat missing rendering as a live applyable hover correction
drill_prompt: Decide whether حَثِيثًۭا needs a wording repair or only rich renderer metadata, and name the next gate.
learner_explanation: A correct-looking word such as “rapidly” is not rich-certified until the token has learner-visible grammar metadata.
nahw_lesson: If the syntactic role is not certified, keep the rich role pending instead of inventing a grammar label.
sarf_lesson: Renderer-only rows still need POS/case/state in the rich metadata, but they do not force a new lemma rule.
what_failed: A string-populated row lacked parse-key/display segments but did not itself prove a fresh sarf/nahw wording defect.
